630ae7fafb0cd6e790c31afb.leadin_sanitized: This novel begins in 1873 Cincinnati several years after Howard and Buglar run away. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this Toni Morrison novel whose protagonist Sethe believes her house is haunted by a child she killed while enslaved.
In this other Morrison novel, Pecola believes Shirley Temple is pretty and she is ugly. This depressing novel’s narrator also tells of the abusive pasts of Pecola’s parents Cholly and Polly.
Morrison also wrote this novel that partially takes part in Michigan where Milkman becomes irritated because humans cannot fly. Later, Milkman tries to rob Pilate only to find rocks and a skeleton.

630ae7fafb0cd6e790c31c92.leadin: This author of The Golden Bowl created a novel where a governess thinks she is haunted by two orphaned children. For 10 points each:
630ae7fafb0cd6e790c31c92.leadin_sanitized: This author of The Golden Bowl created a novel where a governess thinks she is haunted by two orphaned children. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this late 19th century American author of The Turn of the Screw and The Ambassadors.
In this James novel, Catherine Sloper lives in a eponymous New York area with her aunt and father. The father tries to prevent Catherine from marrying partially because he lost his own wife.
In this perhaps more famous James novel, Isabel Archer falls for Gilbert Osmond. Osmond turns out to not be an ideal husband because he is having an affair with Madame Merle.
